target.name: Palantir
target.founder: Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Stephen Cohen, Joe Lonsdale, Nathan Gettings

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 17 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Palantir is a public-stage AI & Software company currently valued at $400B+, having raised $3B+ in pre-IPO capital. The relevant peer universe consists of 1 public comparable (PLTR itself) and 8 private AI & Software firms including Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, Applied Intuition, and Skild AI.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The AI & Software sector contains 17 same-sector peers, but insufficient clustering exists within narrower filters: zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and only one similar-valuation-tier peer emerged from screening. A median derived from the full sector cohort would introduce excessive heterogeneity and misrepresent Palantir's position, given the wide dispersion in business models, revenue maturity, and capital intensity across frontier AI companies.

Without a sufficiently dense peer cohort meeting triangulation criteria, traditional comparable-company valuation cannot be reliably applied. Alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis or transaction precedent—would require forward guidance and historical transaction data not provided here. The current $400B+ valuation reflects public market pricing but lacks corroborating triangulation from private comps or fundamental modeling. Key uncertainties include sustained revenue growth trajectory, path to profitability, and competitive positioning relative to the fragmented private peer set. Further analysis would require either financial projections or a denser comparable set meeting stage and tier criteria.
